comment: Premise-level source/provenance audit against the local arXiv/source-text cache. Each audit-premise from Assumptions.lean is judged individually; group-level approval is not sufficient.

assumption_example1_positive_calibrated_.reason: Example 1 is a two-type probability model with an exponential conditional-value distribution.

hp1 : 0 < p1.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:183-185: a user prefers romance/action with probabilities p1,p2 and p1 > p2
hp1 : 0 < p1.reason: Lean records the nondegenerate probability-domain condition for the two source likelihoods.

hp2 : 0 < p2.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:183-185: a user prefers romance/action with probabilities p1,p2
hp2 : 0 < p2.reason: The model uses p2 as a positive type likelihood in the calibrated two-genre example.

hlambda : 0 < lambda.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:185-186: values are drawn from Exp(lambda); Table 2 also lists lambda > 0
hlambda : 0 < lambda.reason: The positive exponential rate is exactly the source distribution domain.

hp_sum : p1 + p2 = 1.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:183-185: the two genres are the mutually exclusive preference alternatives with probabilities p1,p2
hp_sum : p1 + p2 = 1.reason: Since the example has exactly the two preference types, the two probabilities sum to one.

assumption_finite_discrete_top_value_dom.reason: Theorem 1(i) and its proof use a finite discrete support with a top value and lower support values.

hk_pos : 0 < k.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:65-69 and 321-326: k is the number of consumed/recommended top items
hk_pos : 0 < k.reason: The top-k objective is the source consumption-constraint parameter; Lean exposes the nonempty top-k domain.

hxTop_pos : 0 < xTop.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:55-62 and 2121-2124: item values are user values and x1 is the top support value
hxTop_pos : 0 < xTop.reason: The formal endpoint records the nonnegative/nondegenerate value-domain condition used by the finite-discrete proof.

hxSecond_nonneg : 0 ≤ xSecond.reason: Lean records the lower-support nonnegativity implicit in the nonnegative item-value model.

hsecond_le_top : xSecond ≤ xTop.reason: The non-strict comparison follows from the source ordering of finite support values.

hsecond_lt_top : xSecond < xTop.reason: This is exactly the top-versus-lower support separation used in the proof.

hvalue_le : ∀ ω, value ω ≤ xTop.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:2121-2124: x1 is the top support value of D
hvalue_le : ∀ ω, value ω ≤ xTop.reason: Every sample value is bounded above by the top support value.

hvalue_split : ∀ ω, value ω = xTop ∨ val.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:2121-2124: finite ordered support with top x1 and next/lower values
hvalue_split : ∀ ω, value ω = xTop ∨ val.reason: Every finite-support draw either attains the top value or lies in the lower support.

assumption_positive_type_likelihoods.reason: The paper's finite-type model uses type preference probabilities p_t as likelihood weights and normalizes homogeneity targets by them.

hlike_pos : ∀ t : ItemType T, 0 < likeli.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:55-62: type t is preferred with probability p_t
hlike_pos : ∀ t : ItemType T, 0 < likeli.reason: Lean uses a finite-function representation of the paper's active type likelihoods.

hlike_pos : ∀ t, 0 < likelihood t.reason: Positive likelihoods are the nondegenerate source domain for ratios and gamma-homogeneity normalizers.

hlike_pos : ∀ t, 0 < B.likelihood t.reason: The Bernoulli model uses the same active type-likelihood probabilities.

assumption_bounded_upper_endpoint_densit.reason: Theorem 1(ii) and Lemma 1 use a bounded upper support endpoint, nonnegative value domain, and positive tail constants.

h_base_bounds : ∀ᵐ y ∂baseMeasure, L ≤ y.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:351-366 and 701-713: D has support bounded from above by M; item values are nonnegative
h_base_bounds : ∀ᵐ y ∂baseMeasure, L ≤ y.reason: Lean packages the source bounded-support domain as an almost-everywhere interval bound.

h_nonneg : ∀ᵐ y ∂baseMeasure, 0 ≤ y.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:55-62: conditional item values are user values
h_nonneg : ∀ᵐ y ∂baseMeasure, 0 ≤ y.reason: The nonnegative value domain is part of the recommendation-value model.

hM_pos : 0 < M.reason: The nondegenerate nonnegative bounded-support endpoint is positive in the source distribution class.

hbeta_pos : 0 < beta.reason: This is an exact theorem-statement domain condition.

hc_pos : 0 < c.reason: This is an exact theorem/lemma tail-density condition.

hwidth_pos : 0 < M - L.source_location: Derived from a nondegenerate bounded support interval in Theorem 1(ii)/Lemma 1
hwidth_pos : 0 < M - L.reason: Lean exposes a positive-width interval for the bounded-support measure wrapper.

assumption_exponential_order_statistic_d.reason: Theorem 1(iii) and Lemma D.3 use a positive-rate exponential distribution and positive top-k count.

hlambda_pos : 0 < lambda.reason: This is the exact exponential distribution domain.

hk_pos : 0 < k.reason: The paper's top-k objective uses a nonempty consumption count.

assumption_pareto_finite_mean_domain.reason: Theorem 1(iv) and Lemma D.4 use Pareto alpha > 1 and positive top-k count.

halpha : 1 < alpha.reason: This is the exact finite-mean Pareto domain printed in Theorem 1(iv).

hk : 0 < k.reason: The top-k objective is nonempty in the source model.

assumption_common_mean_argmax_domain.reason: The all-consumed endpoint chooses a type maximizing likelihood when conditional means are common and nonnegative.

hmean_nonneg : 0 ≤ mean.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:55-62 and Theorem 1's finite-mean value model
hmean_nonneg : 0 ≤ mean.reason: Lean exposes the nonnegative common-value domain used by the all-consumed expected-value argument.

hbest : ∀ t, likelihood t ≤ likelihood b.reason: This is the finite-function form of the source argmax condition.

assumption_unique_common_mean_argmax_dom.reason: The converse endpoint uses the nondegenerate positive common mean and a unique likelihood maximizer.

hmean_pos : 0 < mean.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:55-62 and Theorem 1's common finite-mean value model
hmean_pos : 0 < mean.reason: The uniqueness/converse wrapper records the nondegenerate positive common-value case.

hbest_strict : ∀ t, t ≠ best → likelihoo.reason: Lean's strict form is the unique-argmax case of the paper's argmax notation.

hgamma_nonneg : 0 ≤ gamma.reason: This is the exact source quantifier domain.

assumption_decaying_bernoulli_parameter_.reason: Theorem 2 uses decaying Bernoulli success probabilities, with source alpha regimes and nondegenerate Bernoulli probability domains.

halpha_nonneg : 0 ≤ alpha.reason: This is an exact source-domain condition.

halpha_lt_one : alpha < 1.reason: This is the explicit Theorem 2(i) regime.

halpha_gt_one : 1 < alpha.reason: This is the explicit Theorem 2(iii) regime.

halpha : 0 < alpha.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:553-557: Theorem 2(iv) handles alpha >= 0 with alpha=0 separately
halpha : 0 < alpha.reason: Lean's positive-alpha branch is the nonzero subcase of the source all-consumed statement; the alpha=0 endpoint is exposed separately.

hc : 0 < c.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:537-543: q_i = c(i+d)^(-alpha) are Bernoulli success probabilities
hc : 0 < c.reason: Lean records the nondegenerate positive-quality case needed for the paper's homogeneity targets; the source theorem prints c >= 0 but uses a nontrivial success-probability model.

hc_pos : 0 < c.reason: Same nondegenerate decaying Bernoulli quality-domain condition as `hc`.

hc_nonneg : 0 ≤ c.reason: This is an exact source-domain condition.

hd : 0 ≤ d.reason: This is an exact source-domain condition.

hd_nonneg : 0 ≤ d.reason: This is an exact source-domain condition.

hfirst : decayingBernoulliSuccess c d al.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:537-543: X_i are Bernoulli with success probabilities q_i
hfirst : decayingBernoulliSuccess c d al.reason: The Lean first-probability bound is the finite-function domain condition ensuring the printed q_i sequence is a valid Bernoulli success-probability sequence.

hfirst : decayingBernoulliSuccess c d 1.reason: This is the same Bernoulli probability-domain condition specialized to the alpha=1 case.

assumption_varying_bernoulli_probability.reason: Theorem 3 and Corollary 3 use Bernoulli success probabilities in the nondegenerate probability range.

hprob_pos : ∀ t, 0 < B.successProb t.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:605-616: X_i^(t) are Bernoulli(q_t) and the formula uses log(1/(1-q_t))
hprob_pos : ∀ t, 0 < B.successProb t.reason: Lean exposes the positive nondegenerate Bernoulli probability domain required by the source formula.

hprob_lt_one : ∀ t, B.successProb t < 1.reason: The strict upper bound is the well-formed probability/log-domain condition for Theorem 3.

hprob_eq : ∀ i j : ItemType T, B.success.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:518-523: Corollary 3 assumes X_i are Ber(q) with a common q > 0
hprob_eq : ∀ i j : ItemType T, B.success.reason: This is the finite-function form of the i.i.d. Bernoulli/common-success-probability corollary.

assumption_uniform_top_k_positive_count_.reason: Proposition 2 is about positive top-k recommendation/consumption counts along a positive population sequence.

hkpos : ∀ N, 0 < N → 0 < kseq N.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:459-493: Proposition 2 quantifies over top-k counts satisfying the printed k-bound
hkpos : ∀ N, 0 < N → 0 < kseq N.reason: The Lean schedule condition records the nonempty top-k domain for the paper's asymptotic wrapper.

assumption_proposition4_continuous_spher.reason: The folder currently stops Proposition 4 at an averaging/kernel checkpoint; the full continuous sphere, uniform measure, cosine-kernel/Fubini/symmetry, and Laplace-principle analytic instantiation remain a known partial analytic boundary rather than a source caveat.

C : Proposition4ContinuousSphereCertific.reason: The certificate packages the continuous-sphere uniform-measure, symmetry/Fubini, and Laplace analytic ingredients that remain the paper's Proposition 4 partial boundary, not a source caveat.

assumption_lemmaD2_split_integral_analyt.reason: Lemma D.2's finite split-integral assembly is conditional on the near-zero/tail asymptotic split certificate; this is part of the same known partial analytic asymptotic boundary as the continuous/Laplace layer, not a source caveat.

C : BoundedLemmaD2SplitIntegralFiniteCer.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:2181-2205 and Appendix D.2 bounded upper-endpoint derivation
C : BoundedLemmaD2SplitIntegralFiniteCer.reason: The certificate supplies the analytic split-integral asymptotic used by Lemma D.2; the current Lean development has not fully derived that analytic asymptotic from measure/integration primitives, so this remains a partial boundary.

assumption_positive_rounding_population_.reason: Lemma D.5 and finite rounding statements are stated for positive population size.

hNpos : 0 < N.source_location: PRPKG24AccuracyDiversity.txt:65-69 and Lemma D.1/D.5: n/N is the number of recommendations
hNpos : 0 < N.reason: The finite rounding lemmas operate on a positive population/recommendation count.
